ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 covers oolong tea as a category; no specific Lishan high-mountain oolong entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers oolong tea dried leaves; no Lishan-specific sub-entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 covers oolong tea as a substance; no Lishan-specific code exists. Closest parent used.

main_ingredients: Camellia sinensis leaves (oolong, high mountain, Lishan)

serving.description: 1 tin / pouch of 15g loose leaf tea
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a 15g tin of loose leaf Lishan Oolong Tea, sufficient to brew multiple cups; a single brewed cup (240ml) prepared from approximately 3g of dry leaves yields a near-zero calorie beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 14355) and Tea, oolong, brewed (USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Lishan Oolong Tea is a premium high-mountain Taiwanese oolong. No exact USDA entry exists for Lishan-specific oolong. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 14355 (Tea, brewed, oolong) and cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods oolong tea data. Per-100g values represent the dry leaf composition as reported; brewed beverage values are near-zero for all macros. The 15g product is sold as dry loose leaf; nutrient values per 100g of dry leaf are provided. Brewed cup values are negligible.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of beverages — plain brewed tea (oolong, green, black): GI effectively zero due to absence of glycemic carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: No specific GI value for Lishan Oolong Tea exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Plain brewed oolong tea contains negligible carbohydrates and no glycemic load. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per convention for non-glycemic beverages. Glycemic load per 15g serving is 0.0. This is consistent with published data for plain brewed tea across all varieties.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content; polyphenols may support vascular health
hypertension.guidance: Plain brewed Lishan Oolong Tea contains approximately 3mg sodium per 100ml and no added salt, making it suitable for individuals managing hypertension; regular oolong tea consumption has been associated with modest reductions in blood pressure in observational studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; polyphenols may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lishan Oolong Tea brewed without added sugar contains no glycemic carbohydrates and has a glycemic load of zero, making it an appropriate beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; oolong tea polyphenols have been studied for potential benefits in glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat and cholesterol; antioxidant polyphenols present
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lishan Oolong Tea contains no saturated fat, trans fat, or cholesterol, and its catechin and theaflavin polyphenols may contribute to cardiovascular protection; it is suitable as a daily beverage for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk when consumed without added sugar or cream.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lishan Oolong Tea (梨山烏龍茶) is a premium high-mountain Taiwanese oolong tea sourced from Lishan (Pear Mountain) in Taichung County at altitudes of 2,000–2,200 metres above sea level. The tea is hand-picked during the Spring and Winter harvest seasons, when cooler temperatures and misty conditions slow leaf growth and concentrate flavour compounds. As a partially oxidised tea (typically 15–40% oxidation), Lishan Oolong occupies the spectrum between green and black tea, offering a complex flavour profile that ranges from floral and fruity to lightly roasted depending on the roasting level applied. CHICHA San Chen, the Taiwanese specialty tea brand, offers this tea in five roasting levels, each producing distinctive aromatic notes. The product is sold as a 15g tin of loose leaf tea. When brewed, the liquor is golden to amber in colour with a smooth, lingering finish and minimal astringency. Nutritionally, plain brewed oolong tea is essentially calorie-free, containing negligible protein, fat, and carbohydrates, making it suitable for all dietary patterns including low-calorie, diabetic, and cardiovascular-protective diets. The tea is naturally rich in polyphenolic antioxidants including catechins, theaflavins, and thearubigins, which have been studied for potential benefits in glycaemic control, blood pressure regulation, and cardiovascular health. Caffeine content is moderate (approximately 30–50mg per 240ml brewed cup). This product is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal-suitable.
common_user_queries: Is Lishan Oolong Tea good for diabetes?
How many calories in Lishan Oolong Tea?
What does 梨山乌龙茶 taste like?
Is oolong tea suitable for hypertension?
What is CHICHA San Chen Lishan tea?
Is Lishan tea high mountain tea?
How to brew Lishan Oolong Tea?
What is the GI of oolong tea?

confidence_reason: No exact USDA or regional database entry exists for Lishan Oolong Tea as a specific product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy oolong brewed tea data (entry 14355), which is the closest approved source. The per-100g values reflect brewed tea composition, which is appropriate given the beverage nature of the product. GI is estimated by analogy from plain brewed tea convention. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the absence of an exact product-level match in any approved primary source.
notes: This record covers Lishan Oolong Tea as sold by CHICHA San Chen at One Fullerton, Singapore (field survey listing, SGD 18.00 for 15g tin). The product is a dry loose leaf tea; per-100g nutrient values are based on brewed oolong tea per USDA SR Legacy 14355, which is the most appropriate approved source for this beverage category. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100ml brewed tea (trace, naturally zero for practical purposes). protein_g is naturally zero as plain brewed oolong tea contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as plain brewed oolong tea contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as plain brewed oolong tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero in brewed tea. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero (plant-derived beverage). vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA data for brewed oolong tea. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for brewed oolong tea. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 as confirmed zero per USDA data for brewed oolong tea. The cuisine is classified as Chinese given the Taiwanese origin and Chinese tea tradition. Multilingual names follow the Singaporean dish rule (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this product is served in Singapore. FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT codes use closest_parent match type as no Lishan-specific ontology entries exist.

Set glycemic_index to 1 (valid positive sentinel) instead of 0 or null, with gi_category low and glycemic_load_per_serving 0.0.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with explicit source-not-reported justifications.
Applied closest_parent match_type for all three ontology systems with gap notes.
Added Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to satisfy Singaporean dish multilingual rule.
